title: Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles

seo.title: Fideo Seco Recipe: Authentic Mexican Dry Noodles
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Fideo Seco, a delicious Mexican dry noodle dish. This easy recipe brings the taste of Mexico to your kitchen. Perfect for any meal!
longTailKeywords: how to make Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles
easy Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles recipe
authentic Mexican Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles
Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles step by step
best Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles at home

seo.h1: Authentic Fideo Seco: Mexican Dry Noodles Recipe
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Fideo Seco
seo.imageAlt: A close-up shot of a bowl of Fideo Seco, a traditional Mexican dry noodle dish garnished with cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this flavorful Fideo Seco, a classic Mexican dry noodle dish perfect for a family meal.

description: Fideo Seco is a cherished Mexican comfort food, featuring thin vermicelli noodles toasted to golden perfection and then simmered in a rich, vibrant tomato sauce. This 'dry' pasta dish absorbs all the savory liquid, resulting in tender, flavorful noodles. It is often garnished generously with fresh cream, crumbly cheese, and creamy avocado for a truly authentic experience.

ingredients.notes: optional, for deeper color and flavor

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In a small saucepan, bring enough water to a boil to cover the Roma tomatoes. Add the tomatoes, onion, and garlic cloves and boil for about 10 minutes until the tomato skins start to peel and the vegetables are softened. Drain the water and transfer the softened vegetables to a blender.

instructions.title: Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: To the blender with the softened vegetables, add 1 cup of chicken broth, tomato paste if using, salt, and black pepper. Blend until the mixture is completely smooth. You may need to blend in batches if your blender is small. Set aside the vibrant tomato sauce.

instructions.title: Toast the Fideo Noodles
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil in a large, deep skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the fideo noodles and toast them, stirring constantly, until they turn a deep golden brown. Be careful not to burn them, as they cook quickly. This step is crucial for flavor and texture.

instructions.title: Simmer the Fideo in Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully pour the blended tomato sauce into the skillet with the toasted fideo noodles. Stir well to coat the noodles evenly. Add the remaining 2 cups of chicken broth. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.

instructions.title: Cook Until Dry
instructions.text: Once simmering, reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet tightly, and let the fideo cook for about 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid has been absorbed and the noodles are tender. Check periodically to ensure it is not sticking to the bottom.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the skillet from the heat and let it rest, still covered, for 5 minutes. This allows the noodles to fully absorb any remaining moisture and become fluffy. Fluff gently with a fork before serving. Serve the Fideo Seco warm, garnished generously with a drizzle of crema Mexicana, crumbled Cotija cheese, sliced avocado, and fresh chopped cilantro.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, lightly char the tomatoes, onion, and garlic on a comal or dry skillet before blending. This adds a smoky depth to the sauce.
If you prefer a spicier Fideo Seco, add one or two serrano or jalapeño peppers (stemmed and deseeded if you want less heat) to the blender with the tomatoes.
Fideo Seco makes excellent leftovers. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop with a splash of broth if needed.
article: Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles is a beloved Mexican Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
